title: Consilium Traineeships at the Council of the European Union 2026–2027: Paid EU Internship Opportunities in Brussels
summary: Are you looking for a paid European Union traineeship that offers real experience inside EU institutions? title: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program 2026: Paid Global Internships for Students & Recent Graduates | Complete Guide
summary: The Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program offers a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in international development, finance, research, policy, and innovation. Through paid internships at IDB, IDB Invest, and IDB Lab , participants work alongside global experts and contribute to projects that improve lives across Latin America, the Caribbean, Asia, and Europe. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); If you are looking to launch your international career with a respected multilateral organization, this guide explains everything you need to know from eligibility requirements to application tips. Why Choose the IDB Internship Program? The IDB internship program is designed to help students and young professionals: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Gain practical experience in international development Work on real projects with global impact Build professional networks across regions Learn about development finance and policy Experience the organizational culture of the IDB Group Interns work directly with teams at IDB Headquarters in Washington, D.C., or in country offices worldwide, with occasional teleworking flexibility depending on the role. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Types of Internship Opportunities 1. Internships at IDB Headquarters (Washington, D.C.) Interns at headquarters support teams delivering operational and corporate solutions to public and private sector clients. Program Structure Duration: 2 months Work schedule: Full-time (40 hours per week) Format: On-site with some flexibility ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Internship Cycles Winter Internship: January 16 March 15 Summer Internship: June 16 August 15 Applications typically open 3 4 months in advance . Important Note: Applicants may apply for a maximum of three internship positions . 2. Internships at Country Offices (COFs) The IDB has offices across Latin America and the Caribbean, as well as Asia and Europe. These internships are designed to strengthen local development capacity while helping interns build careers in their own regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key Features Duration: 2 to 6 months (based on business needs) No fixed cohort timeline Continuous recruitment throughout the year Eligibility Requirement You must be: A citizen or legal resident of the country where the office is located, or Hold a valid work permit and reside in that country. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Fields of Study in Demand The IDB welcomes students from diverse academic backgrounds, including: Business and Finance Communications and Marketing Data and Technology Economics and Research Environmental Studies Humanities and Social Sciences Law and Policy Linguistic Services Basic Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the IDB Internship Program, applicants must meet the following criteria: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Citizenship Washington, D.C. internships: Must be a citizen of one of IDB s 48 member countries Country Office internships: Must be a citizen or legal resident of the country where applying IDB Invest roles: No citizenship restrictions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Academic Status Active undergraduate student (minimum second year), or Graduate student with at least 50% of coursework completed Availability Must be available to work full-time (40 hours per week) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Additional Requirements Must not have relatives working at IDB, IDB Invest, or IDB Lab Internship must be your first job within the IDB Group ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Required Documents Applicants must prepare the following documents: Updated Resume (CV) University Enrollment Letter (issued within the last 3 months) Academic Transcripts (official or unofficial; A cover letter is only required if specified in the job posting. title: European Forum Alpbach Scholarship Programme 2026 (EFA26) | Fully Funded Opportunity for Global Youth
summary: The European Forum Alpbach Scholarship Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering a fully funded opportunity for students, young professionals, and emerging leaders from around the world to participate in one of Europe s most prestigious interdisciplinary forums. Held annually in the Austrian Alps, the European Forum Alpbach (EFA) brings together future changemakers, policymakers, researchers, and innovators to exchange ideas, build leadership skills, and shape solutions for Europe and the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This scholarship programme is designed for first-time participants who want to gain international exposure, expand their networks, and engage in high-level dialogue across sectors. About the European Forum Alpbach Scholarship Programme Every year, hundreds of young talents travel to Alpbach to take part in a unique two-week experience that combines academic learning, policy dialogue, and cross-cultural exchange. Scholarship holders are at the heart of the Forum and are the only participants who remain for the full duration of the event. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme includes: Scientific, competence-based, and artistic seminars and studios High-level conferences with global decision-makers and experts Leadership and skills development sessions Interdisciplinary discussions and networking opportunities Social and cultural activities designed to foster collaboration Approximately 350 scholarships are awarded annually by the European Forum Alpbach and the Forum Alpbach Network (FAN). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Application Dates for EFA Scholarship 2026 Applicants should take note of the official application period: Applications open: 2 March 2026 Application deadline: 22 March 2026 (11:59 PM CET) Due to the competitive nature of the programme, early preparation is strongly recommended. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why Apply for the European Forum Alpbach Scholarship? The EFA Scholarship Programme offers more than financial support. It provides an immersive leadership experience that helps participants grow professionally and personally while engaging with some of the most pressing issues facing Europe and the world. Key benefits include: Full participation in the European Forum Alpbach event Access to influential policymakers, academics, and industry leaders International networking with peers from diverse backgrounds Opportunities for leadership development and interdisciplinary learning Long-term connection to a global alumni community EFA as a Lifelong Community Participation in the European Forum Alpbach does not end after the event. Scholarship holders become part of the Forum Alpbach Network (FAN), a strong international alumni community dedicated to promoting dialogue and cooperation across borders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The network includes: 34 alumni initiatives Representation across 20 countries Year-round events and collaboration opportunities A growing community committed to shaping a better Europe How to Apply for the EFA Scholarship Programme Applicants can submit their applications through two pathways depending on their regional affiliation. Applications will be open from 2-22 March 2026 (2 pm 11:59 pm CET) Application via the Forum Alpbach Network (FAN) Applicants with nationality or residency ties to specific FAN clubs must apply through their respective alumni club. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); FAN clubs with separate scholarship selection processes include: These are the FAN clubs who offer EFA-scholarships and have a separate selection process: CA Burgenland , CA Liechtenstein , CA Nieder sterreich , CA Poland, CA Upper Austria , CA Salzburg , CA Senza Confini (Carinthia, Northern Italy and Slovenia), CA Steiermark , CA S dtirol Alto Adige , CA Tirol , CA Vorarlberg , IG Wien . Applicants should consult the relevant FAN club website for detailed requirements and deadlines. Application via the European Forum Alpbach (EFA) If there is no FAN club in your country, or your local club does not have its own selection process, you may apply directly via the EFA online application platform. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Important application rules: The scholarship is open to international applicants worldwide, with a special focus on Europe outside Austria Applications must be submitted through the official online portal Incomplete applications will not be accepted Applications submitted by email will not be considered Missing documents submitted after the deadline will not be reviewed ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Special note: Nationals or permanent residents of ROC Taiwan must apply via the Lung Yingtai Cultural Foundation. Documents Required for the Application Applicants should prepare all required documents before beginning the application process. Ensuring completeness and accuracy is essential, as incomplete applications may be automatically rejected. Disqualification Policy and Use of AI The organisers clearly state that applications may be disqualified if: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Minimum eligibility criteria are not met The letter of motivation is generated using artificial intelligence Applicants are encouraged to submit authentic and original application materials. title: LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026: Global AI Workforce Grant Supporting Nonprofits Preparing Young Adults for the AI Economy
summary: The LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026 is a global philanthropic grant initiative designed to help nonprofit organizations prepare young people for a rapidly changing labor market shaped by artificial intelligence. As automation and AI technologies continue transforming industries, LinkedIn is expanding its commitment to economic inclusion by funding organizations that help young adults successfully transition into an AI-powered workforce. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The 2026 edition builds on LinkedIn s first year of funding nonprofit innovators worldwide and introduces an expanded funding round combining financial grants and in-kind support resources. The program focuses specifically on young adults facing structural barriers to employment and aims to ensure that technological transformation does not widen inequality. Why the Future of Work Fund Matters Artificial intelligence is not just creating new jobs; it is reshaping nearly every existing one. According to LinkedIn projections, approximately 70% of the skills required for jobs will change by 2030. This means traditional career preparation models are becoming outdated. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The challenge is not simply training people to use technology. Instead, the workforce now requires a combination of: AI fluency and digital capability Critical thinking and adaptability Communication and collaboration Problem-solving in uncertain environments ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Many young adults particularly those from underserved backgrounds lack access to training and opportunities that help them adapt to this shift. The Future of Work Fund exists to support organizations solving exactly this problem. What the Fund Supports The fund provides both financial grants and in-kind support to nonprofit organizations delivering programs that expand economic opportunity in an AI-driven labor market. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Key Focus Areas Organizations applying must demonstrate a clear and practical approach to at least one of the following: 1. Preparing Young Adults for AI-Driven Careers Programs that teach AI literacy, digital skills, and workforce readiness for modern job markets. 2. Improving Job Access Initiatives helping career starters connect with employment opportunities using technology and AI-enabled pathways. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3. Supporting Workforce Success Training that helps young workers thrive after employment through adaptability, communication, and lifelong learning skills. 4. System-Level Workforce Innovation Projects influencing how education systems, training institutions, or employers integrate AI workforce preparation. What LinkedIn Means by AI Innovation LinkedIn is not only interested in teaching technical coding skills. Instead, the fund prioritizes organizations that combine technological learning with human capabilities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Supported initiatives typically include: AI-fluency training programs Career navigation tools powered by AI Skills-matching platforms Job readiness pathways Human-skill development (communication, collaboration, adaptability) Alternative credential programs Who the Fund Aims to Help The program focuses on young adults entering the workforce who face employment barriers. These may include: Individuals from low-income communities First-generation professionals Learners outside traditional university pathways Underemployed youth Career starters without professional networks ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Typical target age range: 18 24 (varies by country) Eligibility Requirements for Organizations To qualify, applicants must meet all of the following conditions. Organizational Status Must be a legally registered nonprofit, charity, or equivalent not-for-profit organization U.S. organizations must hold 501(c)(3) status Must be able to directly receive grant funding ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Program Focus Organizations must demonstrate: Workforce development programming Clear use of AI innovation OR preparation for AI workforce transition Support for young adults facing economic opportunity barriers Organizations not meeting these requirements are encouraged to use LinkedIn s free nonprofit resources instead. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Funding and Support Structure Selected organizations receive a combination of monetary and strategic assistance. Financial Support Cash grant funding for program implementation and expansion. In-Kind Support Access to LinkedIn expertise Workforce insights and data Platform resources Strategic guidance This hybrid model helps organizations scale impact beyond traditional grants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Evaluation Criteria Applications are reviewed based on: Demonstrated impact potential Clarity of AI integration Capacity to support underserved young adults Scalability of program model Alignment with economic inclusion goals Application Deadline March 15, 2026 at 12 PM PT Submit your application HERE . Visit the LinkedIn Social Impact website HERE for more details. Click HERE to discover more grant opportunities. title: Apply Now: International Law Fellows Network 2026 on the Future of the Rule of Law
summary: The global rule of law is under unprecedented strain. Long-standing models of legal cooperation and governance once shaped by post Cold War optimism are now being challenged by political polarisation, populism, and competing global visions of governance. In response, leading institutions are convening the next generation of legal professionals to rethink how the rule of law can be defended and strengthened in the 21st century. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The International Law Fellows Network 2026: The Future of the Rule of Law in a Fractured World offers a unique opportunity for early- to mid-career lawyers to engage in this urgent conversation and develop practical strategies for safeguarding justice, accountability, and legal integrity worldwide. Why the Rule of Law Matters Now The United States and Europe have historically promoted rule-of-law frameworks through foreign aid, technical assistance, and policy conditionality. However, those approaches are now facing mounting pressure: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); In the United States , political attacks on courts and constitutional structures reveal internal vulnerabilities. Across Europe, democratic institutions are being tested by populism, executive overreach, and disinformation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Globally, rival governance models from Russia and China emphasize sovereignty and centralized control over accountability and individual rights. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); As these trends reshape global governance, rising legal professionals must grapple with how to uphold core principles fairness, equality, transparency, and justice in an increasingly complex world. About the Programme This high-level programme is convened by Salzburg Global Seminar , the International Bar Association (IBA) , and the 21st Century Trust . It brings together early- to mid-career lawyers working in: Major law firms International organizations Public institutions Human rights and advocacy spaces ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Participants will explore how their work in cross-border transactions, compliance, dispute resolution, and human rights law can help defend and adapt the rule of law in a fractured geopolitical landscape. Programme Details Programme Dates: June 11 14, 2026 Location: Schloss Leopoldskron , Salzburg, Austria Application Deadline: April 5, 2026 Fees Funding Fully Funded: Participants selected through IBA member firms or the Salzburg Global Cutler Fellowship community will receive full coverage of programme fees, accommodation, and meals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); IBA-supported participants may also receive travel funding. General Applicants: Programme cost: $4,250 Employer sponsorship is encouraged. Who Should Apply? Ideal Participants Early- to mid-career lawyers with an interest in international law and global governance ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Professionals engaged in cross-border legal practice or policy work Advocates committed to upholding democratic institutions and human rights Programme Goals The International Law Fellows Network aims to: Strengthen leadership among rising international lawyers Equip participants to defend and adapt rule-of-law frameworks Foster transatlantic collaboration on global legal challenges Promote legal systems grounded in accountability, fairness, and rights Key Questions the Programme Will Explore How can lawyers safeguard the rule of law amid geopolitical fragmentation? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); What role do legal professionals play in defending democratic institutions? How can global legal frameworks respond to competing governance models? What practical strategies can strengthen transparency, accountability, and justice worldwide? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply Click HERE , To apply. Deadline: April 5, 2026 In a time when legal norms and democratic institutions are increasingly challenged, the need for principled and forward-thinking legal professionals has never been greater. The International Law Fellows Network 2026 offers a powerful platform for rising lawyers to shape the future of global justice and ensure the rule of law remains a cornerstone of free, fair, and stable societies. If you are ready to contribute to this mission, apply today and become part of a global network committed to defending the rule of law. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); For more Fellowship opportunities, click HERE .
